title: Autacoids: Histamine & Serotonin
summary: الأوتاكويدات مواد طبيعية تُنتج محليًا وتشمل الهيستامين والسيروتونين والبروستاجلاندين. الهيستامين يُطلق من الخلايا البدينة ويعمل على مستقبلات H1 وH2 مسببًا توسع الأوعية، تضيق الشعب الهوائية، وزيادة إفراز حمض المعدة. حاصرات H1 تُقسم إلى جيل أول (مُسكّن، يعبر الحاجز الدموي الدماغي) وجيل ثاني (أقل آثارًا جانبية، لا يسبب نعاسًا). السيروتونين (5-HT) يعمل على عدة مستقبلات: 5-HT1D في الصداع النصفي، 5-HT3 في القيء.

key_drugs.name: Diphenhydramine
key_drugs.note: مُسكّن، ضد الدوار، مضاد للباركنسون المبكر

key_drugs.name: Famotidine
key_drugs.note: أقوى من Cimetidine، لا يثبط إنزيمات الكبد

key_drugs.name: Ondansetron
key_drugs.note: يمنع القيء الناتج عن العلاج الكيميائي (CINV)

mnemonics.title: H1 الجيل الأول: DCC
mnemonics.body:
D = Diphenhydramine (دايف في الدماغ - يعبر BBB)
C = Chlorphenamine (كلور = كثير آثار جانبية)
C = Cyclizine (سيكل = دوار الحركة)
الجميع: يسبب النعاس، يعبر BBB، 3-4 مرات يوميًا

mnemonics.title: H1 الجيل الثاني: CFL
mnemonics.body:
C = Cetirizine (ستار = لا يعبر BBB)
F = Fexofenadine (فاكس = بعيد عن الدماغ)
L = Loratadine (لو = منخفض الآثار الجانبية)
الجميع: لا نعاس، لا يعبر BBB، 1-2 مرات يوميًا

mnemonics.title: Cimetidine: المشكلجي
mnemonics.body: Cimetidine = 'سيمة المشكلجي': يتدخل في كل شيء - يثبط إنزيمات الكبد P450 ويزيد مستوى الأدوية الأخرى (Warfarin)، وعنده تأثير ضد الأندروجين يسبب التثدي عند الرجال.

mnemonics.title: H1 vs H2: طابق أول وثاني
mnemonics.body: تخيل عمارة: الطابق الأول (H1) = الحساسية والنعاس والجلد والشعب الهوائية. الطابق الثاني (H2) = المعدة وإفراز الحمض. كل حاصر يعمل في طابقه فقط.

mnemonics.title: Sumatriptan vs Ondansetron: البطلان
mnemonics.body: Sumatriptan = 'سومو ضرب الصداع': ناهض 5-HT1D يضرب الصداع النصفي مباشرة. Ondansetron = 'اوندا = موجة القيء': يمنع موجة القيء من العلاج الكيميائي بحصار 5-HT3.

flashcards.front: Which receptor is responsible for histamine-induced bronchoconstriction?
flashcards.back: H1

flashcards.front: Which receptor is responsible for histamine-induced increase in gastric acid secretion?
flashcards.back: H2

flashcards.front: What is the main difference between first- and second-generation H1 blockers?
flashcards.back: First generation crosses the BBB and causes sedation; second generation does not cross and is non-sedating

flashcards.front: Name three first-generation H1 drugs
flashcards.back: Diphenhydramine, Chlorphenamine, Cyclizine

flashcards.front: Name three second-generation H1 drugs
flashcards.back: Cetirizine, Fexofenadine, Loratadine

flashcards.front: Which drug is used for motion sickness and pregnancy-related vomiting?
flashcards.back: Diphenhydramine

flashcards.front: What are the anticholinergic side effects of first-generation H1 blockers?
flashcards.back: Dry mouth, urinary retention, blurred vision, tachycardia

flashcards.front: What are the antiandrogenic side effects of Cimetidine?
flashcards.back: Gynecomastia in men, menstrual disturbances in women

flashcards.front: Why is Cimetidine dangerous with Warfarin?
flashcards.back: Because it inhibits hepatic P450 enzymes, increasing Warfarin levels and raising the risk of bleeding

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Sumatriptan?
flashcards.back: Selective 5-HT1D receptor agonist that causes cranial vasoconstriction

flashcards.front: What is the main use of Ondansetron?
flashcards.back: Prevention of chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting (CINV) and postoperative vomiting

flashcards.front: What is the serious side effect of Ondansetron?
flashcards.back: QT interval prolongation

flashcards.front: Where is histamine released from?
flashcards.back: Mast cells in tissues and basophils in the blood

flashcards.front: Which serotonin receptor is associated with anxiety and depression?
flashcards.back: 5-HT1A

flashcards.front: What is the role of 5-HT3 in the body?
flashcards.back: It induces vomiting, especially chemotherapy-induced vomiting

flashcards.front: Where is most of the body's serotonin located?
flashcards.back: In the enterochromaffin cells (90%) of the GI tract

flashcards.front: Which generation of H1 blockers is best for treating seasonal allergies?
flashcards.back: Second generation (safer with fewer side effects)

flashcards.front: Why is Sumatriptan not given to patients with coronary artery disease?
flashcards.back: Because it causes vasoconstriction, which may worsen myocardial ischemia

flashcards.front: What are autacoids?
flashcards.back: Naturally occurring locally acting, short-lived substances that include prostaglandins, histamine, and serotonin.

flashcards.front: Where is histamine secreted from?
flashcards.back: From mast cells in tissues and basophils in the blood; abundant in the lung, skin, and GI tract.

flashcards.front: What are the effects of the H1 receptor?
flashcards.back: Smooth muscle contraction (bronchi), vasodilation, increased permeability, itching and pain - mediator of allergy.

flashcards.front: What are the effects of the H2 receptor?
flashcards.back: Increased gastric acid secretion; blocked by H2 antagonists such as Ranitidine/Famotidine.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between first- and second-generation H1 antagonists?
flashcards.back: First generation (Diphenhydramine) crosses the BBB → sedation and anticholinergic effect; second generation (Loratadine, Cetirizine) does not cross → less sedation.

flashcards.front: What are the effects of serotonin (5-HT)?
flashcards.back: Mood regulation, vascular and intestinal contraction, vomiting (5-HT3), migraine.

flashcards.front: Example of a 5-HT agonist for treating migraine?
flashcards.back: Triptans (Sumatriptan) - 5-HT1 agonists.

flashcards.front: What is the use of 5-HT3 antagonists?
flashcards.back: Ondansetron is an antiemetic, especially with chemotherapy.

mcqs.q: A patient with seasonal allergies needs a non-sedating treatment. Which drug is most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: Cetirizine is a second-generation agent that does not cross the BBB and is non-sedating, making it the best choice for seasonal allergies.

mcqs.q: Which drug is suitable for a patient suffering from motion sickness on a sea voyage?
mcqs.explanation: Cyclizine, a first-generation agent, is used to treat motion sickness.

mcqs.q: A man takes Cimetidine and Warfarin together. What is the expected outcome?
mcqs.explanation: Cimetidine inhibits hepatic P450, reducing Warfarin breakdown and raising its concentration, which increases the risk of bleeding.

mcqs.q: Which drug is used to treat acute migraine?
mcqs.explanation: Sumatriptan is a 5-HT1D agonist and is the first-line treatment for acute migraine.

mcqs.q: A patient has vomiting caused by a chemotherapy session. Which drug is most appropriate?
mcqs.explanation: Ondansetron is a 5-HT3 blocker approved to prevent chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting (CINV).

mcqs.q: What side effects are associated with first-generation H1 blockers (but not second generation)?
mcqs.explanation: First generation is non-selective: it blocks cholinergic and adrenergic receptors, causing dry mouth, tachycardia, and urinary retention.

mcqs.q: Which histamine receptor is responsible for itching and vasodilation?
mcqs.explanation: H1 is responsible for vasodilation, bronchoconstriction, itching, and wakefulness.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is true about Famotidine compared to Cimetidine?
options: Does not inhibit hepatic enzymes and is more potent
mcqs.explanation: Famotidine is more potent and longer acting than Cimetidine, does not inhibit P450, and does not cause androgenic effects.

mcqs.q: Where is serotonin found in the highest concentration in the body?
options: Enterochromaffin cells of the GI tract
mcqs.explanation: 90% of the body's serotonin is in the enterochromaffin cells of the GI tract.

mcqs.q: Why is Diphenhydramine used as a sleeping aid?
options: It crosses the BBB and causes sedation
mcqs.explanation: Diphenhydramine crosses the blood-brain barrier and causes sedation due to central H1 blockade, so it is used as a hypnotic.

mcqs.q: The main cell that secretes histamine in tissues:
First-generation H1 antagonists cause sedation because they:
mcqs.explanation: Their crossing of the blood-brain barrier causes sedation.
A 5-HT3 blocker used as an antiemetic.
